evals.prompt: I have a DSPy program that extracts dates from text. Sometimes the LM returns dates in the wrong format (like 'January 5th' instead of '2024-01-05'). How can I force it to use YYYY-MM-DD format?
evals.expected_output: Code using dspy.Refine with a reward function that validates date format with regex, or Pydantic Field constraints. Mentions that dspy.Assert is removed in DSPy 3.x.

assertions.name: uses_refine_not_assert
assertions.description: Uses dspy.Refine with a reward function or Pydantic validation, not the removed dspy.Assert

assertions.name: validates_date_format
assertions.description: Reward function or validation checks date format with regex or datetime parsing

assertions.name: notes_assert_removed
assertions.description: Mentions that dspy.Assert and dspy.Suggest are removed in DSPy 3.x and recommends Refine or BestOfN

evals.prompt: My DSPy summarization module sometimes produces summaries that are too long. I want it to prefer short summaries but not crash if it goes over. How do I add that as a soft constraint?
evals.expected_output: Code using dspy.Refine with a graduated reward function that penalizes length violations with partial credit. Mentions that dspy.Suggest is removed in DSPy 3.x.

assertions.name: uses_refine_or_bestofn
assertions.description: Uses dspy.Refine or dspy.BestOfN with a graduated reward function, not the removed dspy.Suggest

assertions.name: graduated_reward_not_binary
assertions.description: Reward function gives partial credit for near-misses using graduated float scores, not a binary pass/fail

assertions.name: notes_suggest_removed
assertions.description: Mentions that dspy.Suggest is removed in DSPy 3.x

assertions.name: soft_penalty_not_hard_fail
assertions.description: Explains that the reward function penalizes over-length with a partial score deduction rather than a hard failure

evals.prompt: I have multiple constraints on my DSPy program and it keeps failing. The LM cannot satisfy all 6 rules at once. What should I do?
evals.expected_output: Advice to use a graduated reward function with different weights for hard vs soft rules, and to use dspy.Refine instead of removed dspy.Assert

assertions.name: identifies_joint_constraint_failure
assertions.description: Identifies that too many simultaneous hard constraints reduce joint success probability

assertions.name: recommends_graduated_reward
assertions.description: Recommends using a single graduated reward function with different penalty weights per constraint

assertions.name: differentiates_hard_soft_penalties
assertions.description: Recommends heavy penalties for critical constraints and light penalties for style preferences

assertions.name: recommends_refine_or_bestofn
assertions.description: Recommends dspy.Refine or dspy.BestOfN as the enforcement mechanism, not removed dspy.Assert or dspy.Suggest

assertions.name: does_not_recommend_removed_apis
assertions.description: Does not suggest using dspy.Assert or dspy.Suggest without noting they are removed in DSPy 3.x
